questions.q: Which objectives is Reservation available for? Select 2.
questions.explanation: Only Awareness and Engagement support Reservation. Traffic, Leads, Sales, and App Promotion are Auction only.

questions.q: What are the 3 categories of bid strategies in Meta?
questions.explanation: Spend-based (Highest Volume, Highest Value) spend everything. Goal-based (Cost per Result Goal, ROAS Goal) respect a goal. Manual (Bid Cap).

questions.q: An e-commerce advertiser has a Pixel with value tracking and wants to ensure a minimum ROAS of 4x. Which bid strategy is optimal?
questions.explanation: ROAS Goal defines the target return. Highest Value maximizes value but with no specific return goal. Bid Cap does not guarantee ROAS.

questions.q: A campaign has 4 ad sets with different audiences. You want Meta to allocate more budget to the ad set that converts best. Which setting do you use?
options: Equal daily budget per ad set
Manual lifetime budget per ad set
questions.explanation: Advantage+ Campaign Budget dynamically reallocates to the winning ad set. It requires ≥2 ad sets in the campaign.

questions.q: Which of these DO cause Learning Limited? Select 3.
questions.explanation: A small audience, a low budget, and a restrictive Bid Cap cause Learning Limited (too few optimization events for the system to learn). Pixel + CAPI IMPROVES optimization and Advantage+ campaign budget consolidates spend: neither of those two is a cause.

questions.q: Changing the bid strategy of an ad set in the middle of the learning phase:
options: Resets the learning phase and starts counting events again
questions.explanation: Changing the bid strategy is a significant edit. It resets learning. The ad set starts accumulating 50 events again from 0.

questions.q: Which statement about Bid Cap is TRUE?
options: Bid Cap guarantees a maximum CPA
Bid Cap controls the maximum bid in the auction, NOT the final CPA
Bid Cap is recommended for beginners
Bid Cap only works with Pixel value tracking
questions.explanation: Bid Cap = a ceiling on the auction bid. The final CPA depends on action rates and quality. For a CPA goal, use Cost per Result Goal. Recommended only for advanced users.

questions.q: Up to what % can be shared between ad sets with Ad Set Budget Sharing?
questions.explanation: 20% of the daily budget can be shared between ad sets in the same campaign.

questions.q: Reservation is available on which placements?
questions.explanation: Reservation is limited to FB + IG. Auction allows all placements (including Audience Network, Messenger, Threads).

questions.q: Daily budget vs Lifetime budget: which statement is correct?
options: Daily can never exceed the daily amount
Lifetime is distributed exactly the same every day
Daily is an average (it can exceed ~25% on one day and compensate); Lifetime is an absolute cap over the period
questions.explanation: Daily = a flexible average (exceeds +25% on some days and compensates on others). Lifetime = a cap over the period, with day-to-day variable distribution.

questions.q: A media buyer is deciding between Auction and Reservation for their next campaign. Which of the following is compatible with Auction buying?
options: Scheduling delivery on different days of the week (scheduling)
questions.explanation: Auction allows ad scheduling/dayparting (scheduling by days or hours, using a lifetime budget). Sequenced delivery, predictable CPM, and fixed reach/frequency control are characteristics of Reservation (Reach & Frequency), not Auction.

questions.q: A client gave $100,000 for a 4-week Sales campaign. It spent $40,000 the first week and $20,000 the second. How do you make sure the budget is distributed evenly each week?
questions.explanation: A Daily budget sets an average spend per day, which keeps pacing even week to week. A Lifetime budget can concentrate spend in the highest-opportunity periods and produce uneven weeks like the ones in the scenario. Changing creatives, events, or ad sets does not control pacing.
